name: firecrawl
description: Scrape, crawl, map, and search websites into LLM-ready markdown via the Firecrawl API

tools.name: Scrape Page
tools.description: Scrape a single URL into markdown or other formats

formats.description: Output formats: markdown, html, links, screenshot, json

onlyMainContent.description: Return only the main content, excluding nav/footer boilerplate

tools.name: Search Web
tools.description: Search the web and optionally scrape the results' content

sources.description: Result sources, e.g. [{"type":"web"}]; also supports news and images

historicalSummary.omitted: scraped page content and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Map Site
tools.description: Quickly list the URLs discovered on a website

url.description: Root URL of the site to map

search.description: Filter discovered URLs by a search term

tools.name: Start Crawl
tools.description: Start an asynchronous crawl job over a website (returns a job ID)

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

scrapeOptions.description: Scrape options applied to each page, e.g. {"formats":["markdown"]}

tools.name: Get Crawl Status
tools.description: Get the status and results of a crawl job

id.description: Crawl job ID returned by Start Crawl

prompts.title: Scrape a page
prompts.prompt: Scrape a URL I provide with Firecrawl and summarize its content.

prompts.title: Crawl a site
prompts.prompt: Start a Firecrawl crawl of a website I provide, then check its status and summarize what was found.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Firecrawl API Setup

steps.title: Create a Firecrawl account
steps.description: Go to https://www.firecrawl.dev and sign up — the free plan includes one-time credits for testing.

steps.title: Get your API key
steps.description: Open https://www.firecrawl.dev/app/api-keys and copy your key (or create a new one per project).

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Add it to your .env file as FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the Scrape Page tool on a public URL. A 401 means the key is invalid; a 402 means you are out of credits.

notes: All endpoints consume credits per page; crawls multiply cost by the number of pages — set a small 'limit' first
Start Crawl is asynchronous: poll Get Crawl Status with the returned job ID until status is 'completed'
